title: Cops smash laptop during Pathanamthitta New Year DJ party, DGP calls it power abuse, orders action against DySP
content: The Home Department has initiated a departmental action against Pathanamthitta DySP S Newman following complaints that he had smashed a laptop during a DJ party held as part of New Year celebrations. The event was organised by a club named 'Kammatipadam' on the Geo ground in Pathanamthitta on January 1, 2026. Videos showing tense scenes during the event and a laptop being damaged were widely shared on social media by event participants and DJ Abhiram Sundar. The footage of laptop being kicked and smashed by cops sparked outrage on social media and a few youths had also posted video clips on their pages expressing resentment over alleged police misconduct. According to the police, the celebrations went overboard and tension broke out among youths who were present for the event. Although the police gave repeated instructions to halt the event, the organisers did not pay heed and DJ party went on till 1.45 am. The report further said that police forcefully dispersed the crowd following tension, and one man was injured and taken to Pathanamthitta government hospital. DJ Abhiram Sundar was taken into custody and a case was also registered at Pathanamthitta police station and later Sundar was let off on bail. A preliminary probe showed that Newman entered the stage and kicked at the laptop, causing damage. The order issued by the Home department noted that the police should have seized the equipment if the event progressed, defying the police directive. A letter sent by the state police chief said that causing damage to the gadgets accounted for a serious lapse in duty and caused dishonour to the police force among the public. An oral inquiry has been ordered against Newman as the department observed that his act accounted for irresponsibility, grave abuse of power and negligence of duty.
